codespaces.title: List codespaces in a repository for the authenticated user

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

codespaces.descriptionHTML:
Lists the codespaces associated to a specified repository and the authenticated user.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

name.description: Automatically generated name of this codespace.

display_name.description: Display name for this codespace.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

advanced_security.description:
Enable or disable GitHub Advanced Security for the repository.

For standalone Code Scanning or Secret Protection products, this parameter cannot be used.

dependabot_security_updates.description: Enable or disable Dependabot security updates for the repository.

status.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates for the repository.

reviewers.description: The bypass reviewers for secret scanning delegated bypass

reviewer_id.description: The ID of the team or role selected as a bypass reviewer

reviewer_type.description: The type of the bypass reviewer

custom_properties.description: The custom properties that were defined for the repository. The keys are the custom property names, and the values are the corresponding custom property values.

anyOf.title: Codespace machine
anyOf.description: A description of the machine powering a codespace.

display_name.description: The display name of the machine includes cores, memory, and storage.

operating_system.description: The operating system of the machine.

storage_in_bytes.description: How much storage is available to the codespace.

memory_in_bytes.description: How much memory is available to the codespace.

cpus.description: How many cores are available to the codespace.

prebuild_availability.description: Whether a prebuild is currently available when creating a codespace for this machine and repository. If a branch was not specified as a ref, the default branch will be assumed. Value will be "null" if prebuilds are not supported or prebuild availability could not be determined. Value will be "none" if no prebuild is available. Latest values "ready" and "in_progress" indicate the prebuild availability status.

devcontainer_path.description: Path to devcontainer.json from repo root used to create Codespace.

prebuild.description: Whether the codespace was created from a prebuild.

last_used_at.description: Last known time this codespace was started.

git_status.description: Details about the codespace's git repository.

ahead.description: The number of commits the local repository is ahead of the remote.

behind.description: The number of commits the local repository is behind the remote.

has_unpushed_changes.description: Whether the local repository has unpushed changes.

has_uncommitted_changes.description: Whether the local repository has uncommitted changes.

ref.description: The current branch (or SHA if in detached HEAD state) of the local repository.

location.description: The initally assigned location of a new codespace.

idle_timeout_minutes.description: The number of minutes of inactivity after which this codespace will be automatically stopped.

web_url.description: URL to access this codespace on the web.

machines_url.description: API URL to access available alternate machine types for this codespace.

start_url.description: API URL to start this codespace.

stop_url.description: API URL to stop this codespace.

publish_url.description: API URL to publish this codespace to a new repository.

pulls_url.description: API URL for the Pull Request associated with this codespace, if any.

allowed_port_privacy_settings.description: The privacy settings a user can select from when forwarding a port.

pending_operation.description: Whether or not a codespace has a pending async operation. This would mean that the codespace is temporarily unavailable. The only thing that you can do with a codespace in this state is delete it.

pending_operation_disabled_reason.description: Text to show user when codespace is disabled by a pending operation

idle_timeout_notice.description: Text to show user when codespace idle timeout minutes has been overriden by an organization policy

retention_period_minutes.description: Duration in minutes after codespace has gone idle in which it will be deleted. Must be integer minutes between 0 and 43200 (30 days).

retention_expires_at.description: When a codespace will be auto-deleted based on the "retention_period_minutes" and "last_used_at"

last_known_stop_notice.description: The text to display to a user when a codespace has been stopped for a potentially actionable reason.

codespaces.title: Create a codespace in a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: ref
bodyParameters.description: Git ref (typically a branch name) for this codespace

bodyParameters.name: location
bodyParameters.description: The requested location for a new codespace. Best efforts are made to respect this upon creation. Assigned by IP if not provided.

bodyParameters.name: geo
bodyParameters.description: The geographic area for this codespace. If not specified, the value is assigned by IP. This property replaces location, which is closing down.

bodyParameters.name: client_ip
bodyParameters.description: IP for location auto-detection when proxying a request

bodyParameters.name: machine
bodyParameters.description: Machine type to use for this codespace

bodyParameters.name: devcontainer_path
bodyParameters.description: Path to devcontainer.json config to use for this codespace

bodyParameters.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
bodyParameters.description: Whether to authorize requested permissions from devcontainer.json

bodyParameters.name: working_directory
bodyParameters.description: Working directory for this codespace

bodyParameters.name: idle_timeout_minutes
bodyParameters.description: Time in minutes before codespace stops from inactivity

bodyParameters.name: display_name
bodyParameters.description: Display name for this codespace

bodyParameters.name: retention_period_minutes
bodyParameters.description: Duration in minutes after codespace has gone idle in which it will be deleted. Must be integer minutes between 0 and 43200 (30 days).

codespaces.title: List devcontainer configurations in a repository for the authenticated user

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

codespaces.descriptionHTML:
Lists the devcontainer.json files associated with a specified repository and the authenticated user. These files specify launchpoint configurations for codespaces created within the repository.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

codespaces.title: Get default attributes for a codespace

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The branch or commit to check for a default devcontainer path. If not specified, the default branch will be checked.

parameters.name: client_ip
parameters.description: An alternative IP for default location auto-detection, such as when proxying a request.

codespaces.descriptionHTML:
Gets the default attributes for codespaces created by the user with the repository.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: Response when a user is able to create codespaces from the repository.

statusCodes.description: Response when a user is able to create codespaces from the repository.

codespaces.title: Check if permissions defined by a devcontainer have been accepted by the authenticated user

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The git reference that points to the location of the devcontainer configuration to use for the permission check. The value of ref will typically be a branch name (heads/BRANCH_NAME). For more information, see "Git References" in the Git documentation.

parameters.name: devcontainer_path
parameters.description: Path to the devcontainer.json configuration to use for the permission check.

codespaces.descriptionHTML:
Checks whether the permissions defined by a given devcontainer configuration have been accepted by the authenticated user.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: Response when the permission check is successful

schema.title: Codespaces Permissions Check
schema.description: Permission check result for a given devcontainer config.

accepted.description: Whether the user has accepted the permissions defined by the devcontainer config

statusCodes.description: Response when the permission check is successful
Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.name: codespace_name
parameters.description: The name of the codespace.

codespaces.descriptionHTML:
Triggers an export of the specified codespace and returns a URL and ID where the status of the export can be monitored.

If changes cannot be pushed to the codespace's repository, they will be pushed to a new or previously-existing fork instead.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

codespaces.descriptionHTML:
Publishes an unpublished codespace, creating a new repository and assigning it to the codespace.

The codespace's token is granted write permissions to the repository, allowing the user to push their changes.

This will fail for a codespace that is already published, meaning it has an associated repository.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.name: owner

secrets.descriptionHTML:

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the codespace or codespace:secrets scope to use this endpoint.

selected_repositories_url.description: The API URL at which the list of repositories this secret is visible to can be retrieved
